reasoning: The current 10-year Treasury yield is around 4.10%, having trended downward recently over the last six months (down from a high of around 4.5% in early 2025). The FRED statistical linear model suggests a low probability (0.27) of the value being above 4.10% by mid-December, though adjusting for a seasonal end-of-year uptick brings the combined quantitative estimate to around 0.47. Qualitative expert forecasts for late 2025 and 2026 expect yields to hover around 4.00% to 4.14%, reflecting a balance of Fed rate cuts against persistent inflation and debt concerns. Given the recent downward momentum, I forecast a modest probability of an increase by December 14 (41%), with the probabilities gradually shifting closer to 50% for January (45%) and March (48%) 2026 as the forecast horizon and uncertainty increase over time.
